comment_content:
I'm not gonna lie, after watching season 2 this is how it is:

Season 1 felt tight. the fights were brutal but clean, and the story had a sharp focus on Yeon Si-eun’s growth and his friendships. Every scene had weight.

Season 2 kinda lost that precision. It had awesome moments, but it was juggling too many characters and subplots, so the emotional punches didn’t land as hard. It felt more chaotic than intense sometimes.

I'm hoping we the fanbase remain strong and positive though so we can get a season 3.
if we give criticism or harsh criticism, netflix will just drop it because they're brain-rotted mutts with 0 abilities or skills who just do random shit. This is proven with the stupid shit they greenlight and give continuations to then the amazing shit they just cut off (when it was their fault that it did poorly, they usually just suck ass at advertising when it's a good show or movie.)


Season 2 definitely didn't have the soul that season 1 had, it was lackluster, but at the end of the day I loved the fights and I loved how it ended, I'm really hoping for a season 3, it felt like they were just trying to get the webtoon's storyline out of the way so they can go off in their own direction. It was definitely nowhere near what the webtoon was, nor did it have all of the characters, maybe we meet them in season 3? anyways, praying for season 3, can't let it end like this, season 1 showed us the potential that this masterpiece of a director can create.
